stories.headline: Senate impeachment court defers decision on subpoena for Duterte financial records
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court deferred deciding whether to subpoena Vice President Sara Duterte's bank and tax records after prosecutors and defense lawyers disagreed over accountability versus privacy, the Philippine Daily Inquirer reported. Prosecutor Chel Diokno said the records are needed to establish the true state of her finances, while defense lawyer Michael Poa questioned the relevance of documents dating to 2007 and opposed subpoenaing her husband, a private citizen. Court spokesperson Reginald Tongol said President Marcos may need to authorize the release of her tax records.
stories.context: Duterte, the first Philippine official to be impeached twice, is on trial before the Senate over alleged misuse of confidential funds, undisclosed wealth and threats against President Marcos and others; the trial proper began July 6 and the Senate has allotted 92 trial days. In the 2012 impeachment of Chief Justice Renato Corona, then-President Aquino authorized the release of financial records.
stories.watch_for: Prosecutors said they plan to present evidence on the embezzlement charges next week.

stories.headline: Pro-Marcos vlogger killed, partner wounded in Caloocan shooting
stories.summary: A woman who identified herself online as a supporter of President Marcos was shot dead and her partner wounded in Caloocan City, police said, according to the Philippine Daily Inquirer. A neighbor found her unconscious with a head wound after hearing gunshots, and her partner was taken to a hospital. Investigators said they were pursuing suspects seen riding a motorcycle and had not established a motive, including whether the killing was linked to her online activity.

stories.headline: Army recovers two NPA weapons caches in Surigao del Sur and Bukidnon
stories.summary: The Philippine Army said it recovered firearms and ammunition from two separate New People's Army caches in Mindanao, according to the Philippine Daily Inquirer. Troops found three AK-47 rifles with defaced serial numbers on July 13 in San Miguel, Surigao del Sur, and an M4A1 carbine, a .30-caliber carbine, magazines, 186 rounds of ammunition and two backpacks on July 4 in Malaybalay City, Bukidnon. Lt. Col. Joe Patrick Martinez said the recoveries disrupted the insurgents' logistics as the military works to end remaining armed threats in the Caraga region.

stories.headline: LTFRB caps taxi app booking fees at P60
stories.summary: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board approved a memorandum circular capping booking and platform fees for taxis hailed through digital apps at P60, or 20 percent of the approved fare, whichever is lower, GMA News reported. The rules bar unauthorized, overlapping and undisclosed charges and require operators to disclose all fare components before booking. LTFRB chairman Vigor Mendoza said violators face fines, suspension or cancellation of their franchise. The circular takes effect after publication in a newspaper of general circulation.

stories.headline: Government begins distributing expanded UPLIFT cash aid to 7.5 million households
stories.summary: The Department of Social Welfare and Development began distributing the expanded UPLIFT cash assistance to a targeted 7.5 million households, GMA News reported. About 3.5 million current 4Ps and Walang Gutom members receive a one-time P2,000, while 2.5 million poor households and 1.5 million lower-income Social Security System members are to receive P2,000 monthly through December. DSWD Assistant Secretary Irene Dumlao said the rollout covers all three beneficiary groups.
stories.context: President Marcos expanded the program in response to rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict; the Department of Budget and Management released P12.375 billion to fund it.
stories.watch_for: Monthly payments to the second and third beneficiary groups are scheduled to run through December 2026.

stories.headline: Senators demand apology from China over AI video mocking Filipinos
stories.summary: Several senators condemned an AI-generated video published by Chinese state outlet China Daily that depicted a monkey in a barong being manipulated by arms labeled Japan and the US and then hit with a water cannon, GMA News reported. Senator Risa Hontiveros called the video appalling and said the Chinese government owes Filipinos a public apology, while Panfilo Lacson, Francis Pangilinan and Juan Miguel Zubiri also criticized it; Senator Robin Padilla said a Philippine Coast Guard spokesperson had helped provoke the exchange. The Philippines has filed a diplomatic protest over the material.
stories.context: The video appeared around the 10th anniversary of the 2016 arbitral ruling that rejected most of China's South China Sea claims, a decision Beijing does not recognize. Manila lodged a formal protest earlier in the week, and the Department of National Defense said dozens of countries have backed the ruling.

stories.headline: Large fuel price increases expected on July 21 amid Middle East tensions
stories.summary: Local oil companies are expected to raise pump prices sharply on July 21, with an industry source cited by GMA News estimating diesel could rise by P9.50 to P10.50 per liter and gasoline by P3.50 to P4.50. The source attributed the increases to an elevated geopolitical risk premium following Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz and US retaliatory strikes, sharply reduced vessel traffic through the waterway, and lower Russian diesel exports. Final adjustments depend on end-of-week market averages.
stories.watch_for: Oil firms typically announce final price adjustments early in the week for implementation on Tuesday, July 21.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings rain to northern Luzon; no cyclone in PAR
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon was affecting the western section of northern Luzon as of its 4 p.m. bulletin, bringing scattered rains and thunderstorms over the Ilocos region and Zambales and isolated showers over the Cordillera, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands. The rest of the country, including Metro Manila, can expect partly cloudy skies with isolated afternoon thunderstorms. The agency warned of possible flash floods or landslides during heavier downpours and said no tropical cyclone was inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility.

stories.headline: Metro Manila minimum wage to rise by P85 in two tranches
stories.summary: The P85 daily minimum wage increase for Metro Manila will be released in two tranches, with P60 taking effect on July 25 and the remaining P25 on January 20, 2027, the Manila Bulletin reported, citing Labor Secretary Francis Tolentino. The adjustment will bring the daily minimum to P780 for most workers and P743 for certain smaller establishments. Tolentino said the July 25 start was moved from an earlier July 19 date because of weather disruptions and newspaper publication requirements.
stories.watch_for: The first P60 tranche takes effect July 25, 2026, and the remaining P25 on January 20, 2027; wage boards in Calabarzon, Central Luzon and Central Visayas are deliberating similar increases.

stories.headline: Alas Pilipinas men fall to Indonesia, miss SEA V Cup semifinals
stories.summary: Indonesia swept the Alas Pilipinas men's volleyball team 25-20, 25-23, 27-25 at the Candon City Arena, topping Pool A at 2-0 and eliminating the Philippines from semifinal contention, the Manila Bulletin reported. The loss dropped the young Philippine squad to 0-2 in pool play, sending it to the classification round for fifth place against Myanmar. Joshua Magalaman scored 13 points and Jude Garcia added 10 for the Philippines.
stories.context: The Philippines fielded a largely inexperienced lineup, with only four players from last year's World Championship team; coach Angiolino Frigoni said the priority was developing the squad ahead of the Asian Games.
stories.watch_for: The Philippines is set to face Myanmar in the classification round for fifth place.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes for a seventh straight night
stories.summary: US strikes on Iran continued into a seventh consecutive night, and Iran's health ministry said attacks since July 6 have killed at least 50 people and wounded more than 500, Al Jazeera reported. Iran's national water company said overnight strikes cut water to about 10,000 people across 20 villages in the country's south. The report said the two sides continued to exchange fire as mediators pressed for a return to negotiations.
stories.context: The conflict began in late February when the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran; a June memorandum of understanding briefly paused the fighting before it resumed. The war has repeatedly disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, which normally carries about a fifth of the world's oil and gas.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia kill at least eight
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone attacks on western Russia killed at least eight people and wounded dozens, striking logistics warehouses in the Tambov and Moscow regions and an oil depot near Noginsk, Al Jazeera reported. A regional governor said seven night-shift workers died when drones hit a logistics center, while President Volodymyr Zelenskyy described the targeted warehouses as significant facilities supplying components for drone production. Al Jazeera's correspondent said it was among the highest tolls from Ukrainian strikes on Russia in three years.

stories.headline: Tech stocks slide as new low-cost Chinese AI model rattles investors
stories.summary: Shares of Nvidia and other major technology companies fell sharply after Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a low-cost model it says can rival leading US systems, Nikkei Asia reported. Investors worried that Chinese developers are closing the technology gap while offering AI services at much lower prices. The selloff spread to Asian markets, with Japanese chipmaker Kioxia dropping 16 percent.
